quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Midterm Review Quiz 7
quiz.subtitle: Comprehensive Review — Kinematics through Simple Machines
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question: A stunt driver accelerates a car from an initial speed of $11.4\text{ m/s}$ along a straight runway. If the vehicle covers a total distance of $132\text{ m}$ in exactly $6.80\text{ s}$, calculate the uniform acceleration maintained by the car during this run.

An environmental sensor tracks water flow through a filtration channel over an extended observation period lasting exactly $0.0450\text{ weeks}$. Convert this measured time frame directly into seconds ($\text{s}$).

A block attached to a string is swung in a vertical circle. As the block moves from the lowest point of the circle upward toward the highest point, analyze the work done on the block by the tension force from the string, assuming the string length remains perfectly constant.
questions.explanation: Tension always acts toward the center, perpendicular to the block's tangential displacement. $W = Fd\cos90° = 0$.

questions.question: A $72.0\text{ kg}$ hiker scrambles up a mountain trail. The hiker's gravitational potential energy increases by $1.85 \times 10^5\text{ J}$ during this climb. Calculate the total vertical height gained. ($g = 9.80\text{ m/s}^2$)

A mechanical test hammer delivers a heavy horizontal strike to an industrial safety barrier, transferring $840\text{ J}$ of kinetic energy into it. The barrier deforms plastically by $0.0750\text{ m}$ along the line of impact while bringing the hammer to rest. Calculate the average resistive force exerted by the barrier.

A $1.45\text{ kg}$ metal block sliding along a low-friction tracking rail experiences a sudden magnetic braking pulse. The pulse applies an average retarding force of $320\text{ N}$ opposing the motion for $0.0340\text{ s}$. Calculate the total reduction in the block's linear velocity caused by this braking action.

An industrial ventilation fan assembly uses an electric motor to exhaust air through a duct network. The motor performs $5.12 \times 10^5\text{ J}$ of useful mechanical work every $4.00$ minutes to keep the air moving at a steady rate. Determine the average power output of the fan assembly.

A mechanical gear assembly delivers $2,150\text{ J}$ of useful mechanical work to a conveyor line while operating at an efficiency rating of $82.0\%$. Calculate the amount of energy that was lost to friction and dissipated as heat within the gear housing.

A $0.0125\text{ kg}$ projectile traveling horizontally at $340\text{ m/s}$ passes completely through a stationary $3.50\text{ kg}$ block of dense insulation foam. The projectile emerges from the opposite side at $120\text{ m/s}$ along its original line of motion. Calculate the resulting recoil speed of the foam block on a frictionless surface.

A logistics worker uses a wheelbarrow to transport a heavy load of gravel. The combined center of gravity acts as a downward load of $850\text{ N}$ located $0.420\text{ m}$ from the wheel's axle pivot. The worker lifts upward on the handles at $1.35\text{ m}$ from the axle. Calculate the minimum input effort force required to balance the wheelbarrow.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You're well-prepared across all units!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the topics where you made errors.
scoreMessages.50: Keep studying! Focus on kinematics with initial velocity, work by tension, and projectile-through-block collisions.
scoreMessages.0: Significant review needed. Revisit kinematics, energy, momentum, and simple machines.
